_note: Distilled frameworks and heuristics derived from the spec's reading list, encoded in original wording as machine-usable rules. Facts and trading heuristics, not reproduced text. The signal engine and selector consume these; the learning loop reweights them.

rules: Trend is primary: trade with the direction of the 200-day MA; counter-trend trades get reduced size and tighter stops
Volume confirms price: breakouts on >1.5x average volume are trustworthy; breakouts on light volume are suspect
Support/resistance flip roles after a clean break; the 50-day MA acts as dynamic support in uptrends
Divergence between price and momentum (RSI/MACD) precedes reversals more reliably than overbought/oversold levels alone
Reversal candles only matter at extremes after a trend, never mid-range
Engulfing patterns and hammers at support with volume = highest-probability reversal signals
Doji after extended trend = indecision; wait for the next bar's confirmation before acting
Gaps in the direction of trend tend to fill in range-bound regimes and run in trending regimes
Measured-move targets: project the pattern height from the breakout point
Throwbacks/pullbacks to the breakout level occur in roughly half of breakouts; they are entries, not failures, if volume holds
Tight consolidation before breakout beats loose consolidation; volatility contraction precedes expansion
Buy new 52-week highs out of sound bases on volume, never falling knives
Hard stop 7-8% below entry, no exceptions, no averaging down
Market direction filter: most breakouts fail when the index is below its 50-day MA
Match strategy to BOTH direction and volatility view: direction picks call/put side, IV rank picks debit vs credit structure
Defined-risk spreads over naked positions whenever account constraints or uncertainty are present
Roll or close at 21 DTE for short premium to dodge gamma risk into expiry
Take profits mechanically: 50% of max profit on credit trades outperforms holding to expiry on a risk-adjusted basis
IV rank > 50 favors selling premium; IV rank < 30 favors buying it; 30-60 favors spreads that mute vega
IV is mean-reverting; realized vol is the anchor IV reverts toward
Term structure normally slopes upward; inversion (front month above back) signals stress -- stop selling premium
Skew: OTM puts carry persistent IV premium versus calls; put spreads partially harvest the skew you pay for
Earnings IV crush: front-month IV collapses after the print; never hold long premium through a binary event you bought ahead of
Theta is the rent: long options must have a catalyst or trend strong enough to outrun daily decay
Gamma explodes inside 7 DTE; short premium there demands smaller size and faster exits
Portfolio-level greeks matter more than per-position: cap net delta and keep theta positive when in premium-selling mode
Vega risk concentrates in longer-dated options; calendars are long vega, condors short vega

backtesting_pitfalls.source_style: Lopez de Prado / Chan rigor rules
rules: Overfitting is the default outcome: every added parameter must justify itself out-of-sample
Survivorship and look-ahead bias inflate every naive backtest; use point-in-time data and walk-forward splits
A strategy needs an economic rationale (who is on the other side and why) before statistics mean anything
Meta-labeling: use the primary signal for direction and a second model for bet size
Minimum sample: do not trust a setup with fewer than ~20 independent historical occurrences
Separate alpha model, risk model, transaction-cost model, and portfolio construction; never let one component silently do another's job
Small, consistent edges compound; the risk model is what keeps you alive long enough to collect them
Cut losses fast, let winners run; asymmetry of outcomes matters more than win rate
Position size so that no single trade can knock you out of the game
The crowd is right during trends and wrong at extremes; sentiment extremes are contrarian signals
Sitting on hands is a position: when the setup is not there, do not trade
After a losing streak, cut size; after a winning streak, do not increase it euphoric-fast
Correlations go to 1 in a crisis: diversification measured in calm markets overstates protection
Leverage converts a drawdown into a death sentence; this system uses none
Models calibrated on recent history underestimate tail frequency; circuit breakers must be dumb and absolute, not model-driven

fama_french_1992.finding: Value and size factors explain cross-sectional returns beyond market beta
fama_french_1992.use: factor tilt awareness in equity selection

jegadeesh_titman_1993.finding: 3-12 month winners keep winning over the next 3-12 months

heston_1993.finding: Stochastic volatility model with closed-form pricing; vol clusters and mean-reverts
heston_1993.use: IV mean-reversion assumption in premium-selling gates

carr_wu_2009.finding: Variance risk premium: implied vol systematically exceeds realized; selling premium has structural edge with tail risk
carr_wu_2009.use: why iron condors at high IV rank are the default income trade

welch_goyal.finding: Most published equity-premium predictors fail out-of-sample
welch_goyal.use: skepticism prior on any single macro predictor; ensemble signals only

george_hwang.finding: Proximity to 52-week high predicts returns; anchoring drives underreaction
george_hwang.use: 52-week-high breakout rule in the equity playbook
